title: Selectable advantage linkage mechanism for hand operated tools
abstract: Disclosed herein is a tool operable through a hand squeezing force applied, in use of the tool, by an operator between handgrips of first and second handle members. The tool includes a first application member supported on the first handle member, a second application member supported for reciprocal displacement relative to the first application member, and a linkage mechanism coupling the second handle member to the second application member. The linkage mechanism includes a user selectable means for adjusting the mechanical advantage of force, in use, transferred from the handgrips to the application members.
claims: Claims 1 . A tool operable through a hand squeezing force applied, in use of the tool, by an operator between handgrips of first and second handle members, the tool including: a first application member supported on the first handle member; a second application member supported for reciprocal displacement relative to the first application member; and a linkage mechanism coupling the second handle member to the second application member, the linkage mechanism including a user selectable means for adjusting the mechanical advantage of force, in use, transferred from the handgrips to the application members. 2. A tool as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the linkage mechanism includes a lever arm coupled to the first handle member at a lever arm pivot, the lever arm being coupled on one side of the lever arm pivot to the second application member and on the other side of the lever arm pivot to the second handle member. 3. A tool as claimed in claim 2, wherein the lever arm is coupled to the second handle member by a push arm that is pivotally supported by the second handle member. 4. A tool as claimed in claim 3, wherein a position of engagement of the push arm with the lever arm is governed by a user operable selector, whereby operation of the selector adjusts the mechanical advantage of force, in use, transferred from the handgrips to the application members. 5. A tool as claimed in claim 4, wherein the user operable selector includes a rotatable cam member that bears against the push arm to govern the position of engagement with the lever arm. 6. A tool as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the first application member includes a nose piece for receiving a rivet and the second application member includes a mandrel gripping means, and whereby the reciprocal displacement of the first and second application members is a lateral movement of the nose piece with respect to the mandrel gripping means. 7. A tool as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the second application member is pivotally coupled to the first handle member at an application member pivot, and whereby the reciprocal displacement of the first and second application members is a cutting movement. 8. The tool as claimed in claim 7, wherein the linkage mechanism includes a push arm, one end of the push arm being pivotally supported by the second handle member and the other end of the push arm engaging the second application member on a distal side of the application member pivot. 9. The tool as claimed in claim 8, wherein a position of engagement of the push arm with the second application member is governed by a user operable selector, whereby operation of the selector adjusts the mechanical advantage of force, in use, transferred from the handgrips to the application members. 10. A tool as claimed in claim 9, wherein the user operable selector includes a rotatable cam member that bears against the push arm to govern the position of engagement with the second application member. 1 1 . A tool as claimed in any one of claims 7 to 10, wherein the first and second application members include opposing cutting blades, and whereby the reciprocal displacement of the first and second application members is a cutting movement of the opposing cutting blades. 12. A rivet setting tool including a first handle member with a handgrip toward one end thereof and a first application member toward the other end, a second application member supported for reciprocal sliding displacement with respect to the first application member, a second handle member having a handgrip toward one end thereof and a pivotal coupling to the first handle member toward the other end, and a linkage mechanism coupling the second handle member to the second application member, the linkage mechanism including a user selectable means for adjusting the mechanical advantage of force, in use, transferred from the handgrips of the first and second handle members to the first and second application members. 13. A rivet setting tool as claimed in claim 12, wherein the linkage mechanism includes a lever arm coupled at a lever arm pivot to the first handle member, the lever arm being coupled on one side of the lever arm pivot to the second application member and on the other side of the lever arm pivot to the second handle member. 14. A rivet setting tool as claimed in claim 13, wherein the lever arm is coupled to the second handle member by a push arm that is pivotally supported by the second handle member. 15. A rivet setting tool as claimed in claim 14, wherein a position of engagement of the push arm with the lever arm is governed by a user operable selector by means of which the mechanical advantage of the tool can be adjusted. 16. A rivet setting tool as claimed in claim 15, wherein the selector includes a rotatable cam member that bears against the push arm to govern the engagement position with the lever arm. 17. A hand tool including: a first handle supporting first and second application members for displacement relative to one another to effect a clamping action; a second handle pivotally mounted in relation to the first handle to enable user operation of the tool by squeezing the first and second handles together; and a linkage mechanism coupling the second handle to the first and/or second application members whereby user operation of the first and second handles causes relative displacement of the first and second application members, the linkage mechanism including a user selectable means for adjusting the mechanical advantage of the tool. 18. A hand tool operable by squeezing action of first and second handles for effecting a clamping action through relative displacement of first and second tool application members, the tool including a linkage mechanism coupling the second handle to the first and/or second clamping members, the linkage mechanism including a user selectable means for adjusting the mechanical advantage of forces transferred from the handles to the application members.
